provisions.title: Short title
provisions.content: This Act may be cited as the Office of Attorney-General Act 1994.

provisions.title: Principal legal advisor
provisions.content: The Attorney-General is the principal legal adviser to the Government of Lesotho.

provisions.title: Functions of the Attorney-General
provisions.content: In addition to the duties vested in the Attorney-General by the Constitution of Lesotho, the Attorney-General shall represent the Government of Lesotho in all legal proceedings in which the Government is a party.

provisions.title: Defence of matter at public expense
provisions.content: (1) Where the Attorney-General is of the opinion that it is in the pub­lic interest to do so, he may director that any matter before a court be defended at the expense of the State. (2) a direction under subsection (1) is authority for the withdrawal of money from the Consolidated Fund for that purpose.

provisions.title: Staff
provisions.content: (1) In the exercise of his functions, the Attorney-General shall be assisted by staff appointed or employed under the Public Service Order 1970. (2) For the purposes of the Public Service Order 1970, the staff of the Attorney-General shall be regarded as a department called the Attorney-General’s Chambers.

provisions.title: Legal cadre
provisions.content: (1) Public officers whose duties include the provision of legal servic­es to the Government or government agencies shall at all times be accountable to the Attorney-General in the discharge of those duties. (2) In respect of the provision of legal services to the Government or government agencies, the Attorney-General may issue general or special instructions to those officers referred to in subsection (1).

provisions.title: Repeal
provisions.content: The Office of Attorney-General Act 1985 1 and the Office of Attorney-General Order 1990 2 are repealed. 1 Act No. 6 of 1986 2 Order No. 15 of 1990 Notes The explanatory memorandum to the Office of the Attorney-General Bill 1994 that was introduced into the National Assembly on 4th May 1994 is published as Government Notice No. 48 of 1994 .
